title: ForgedTech Agent Standards
description: Per-repo rules and context that AI agents must read before making changes.

version.description: Schema version. v2 adds tier metadata on rules; plain strings still accepted as legacy form (tier: practice).

extends.description: Default(s) to inherit from. Currently supports built-in: 'forgedtech/org-defaults'. Project values win on scalars; arrays concatenate and dedupe.

repo.description: Repo name in 'org/repo' form. Used by the MCP to confirm correct file.

language.description: Primary language. Selects which CI quality gate applies.

models.description: Model routing per task phase. Logical names (planning / execution) so we don't hardcode model IDs that change. CLAUDE.md maps them to concrete IDs at the boundary. Project files override org defaults via normal merge.

planning.description: Used for design, hypothesis-first task definition, architecture decisions. Default: opus / medium effort.

execution.description: Used for writing the planned code, fix loops, mechanical refactors. Default: sonnet.

gates.description: Project-level workflow gate toggles. Canary projects opt in here before org-wide rollout.

definition_of_ready.description: Definition-of-ready gate (Increment 2). When enabled, start_task with phase='execution' requires scope_statement, files_intended, test_approach, definition_of_done, and out_of_scope. size='trivial' bypasses but is logged.

required_fields.description: Override default required fields. Defaults to all five if omitted.

scope_expansion.description: Scope-expansion gate (Increment 3). When enabled, propose_change blocks paths not matching the active task's files_intended. The agent must call expand_scope (with user confirmation) to add the path before retrying. Depends on definition_of_ready producing files_intended.

auth_change_asvs_artifact.description: Auth-change ASVS artifact gate (Increment 5). When enabled, propose_change against an auth path blocks unless attach_asvs_review has been called on the active task. Artifact records which ASVS L1 controls were touched and what verification was done. Replaces 'mental review' with an audit trail.

paths.description: Glob patterns for auth-sensitive paths. Defaults to ['**/auth/**', '**/permissions/**', '**/session/**'].

surface_uncertainty.description: Surface-uncertainty gate (Increment 8.5). When enabled, the surface_uncertainty MCP tool records ambiguity / unknown deps / conflicting rules / unexpected state on the active task. In strict mode (default_mode='block' or project listed in strict_mode_projects), propose_change blocks until each uncertainty is resolved via surface_uncertainty({ resolve: {...} }).

default_mode.description: log_only: records but doesn't block. block: propose_change halts until uncertainties are resolved.

strict_mode_projects.description: Project slugs that always run in block mode regardless of default_mode.

bugfix_root_cause.description: Bugfix root-cause gate (Increment 8.5). When enabled, start_task with task_type='bugfix' requires a plausible root_cause hypothesis (≥10 chars, not 'unknown'/'tbd').

stack.description: Concrete tooling. Helps the agent pick the right commands.

style.description: Coding style rules. Plain strings (legacy) or tiered objects. Read by the agent at task start.

style_ui.description: UI-specific style rules. Loaded ONLY when the project's ci.kind is 'mobile' or 'web'. Skipped on services / libraries / Workers to save tokens.

rules.description: Architectural constraints. Plain strings (legacy) or tiered objects.

rules_ui.description: UI-specific architecture rules. Loaded ONLY when the project's ci.kind is 'mobile' or 'web'.

feature_path_pattern.description: Glob describing where new features live, e.g. 'apps/*/Features/<Name>/'.

sensitive_paths.description: Paths where 'hard mode' applies — agent must justify changes against the recorded hypothesis. Glob patterns.

tenant_isolation.description: Multi-tenant isolation invariant (Increment 7). When configured, check_tenant_isolation verifies every function in data_layer_paths accepts tenant_id_field as a parameter. Inline bypass: '// tenant-isolation: bypass <reason>'.

tenant_id_field.description: Parameter name that identifies the tenant (e.g. 'householdId' for Kurata, 'accountId' for TradingBot).

data_layer_paths.description: Glob patterns for files containing tenant-scoped queries. e.g. ['packages/api/src/data/repos.ts', 'packages/api/src/data/drizzle.ts']

bypass_comment_pattern.description: Regex matching the inline bypass directive. Defaults to '// tenant-isolation: bypass'.

exempt_methods.description: Method names that are legitimately tenant-free (e.g. 'create' on the tenant entity itself, cross-tenant scanner queries). Each entry should ALSO carry a bypass comment in source; this is a project-level allowlist for repeated patterns.

regression_required_for.description: Path globs that require an accompanying regression test on change.

notes.description: Free-form notes about the project's testing situation — baselines, known-broken suites, etc.

per_surface.description: Per-glob coverage thresholds. Keys are path globs; values are thresholds (statements / branches / functions). Stricter than the top-level unit_min on the matched paths.

excluded.description: Path globs excluded from coverage. Pair with excluded_rationale to document WHY.

excluded_rationale.description: One-line justifications for entries in `excluded`. Inflating coverage by excluding hard-to-test code is a smell — always justify.

rules.description: Project-specific testing rules (e.g. 'service tests are mandatory', 'pure helpers must have unit tests before merge').

exempt_from_test_requirement.description: Path globs where new code does NOT require a test. Use sparingly — typically only generated files, third-party adapters, or storybook entries.

explicit_approval_required_for.description: Path globs where the agent must pause for user approval before writing.

investigation.description: Controls the hypothesis-first protocol enforced by mcp.start_task / mcp.propose_change.

mode.description: soft: hypothesis recorded but not blocking. hard: propose_change blocks if written files weren't read during investigation.

min_read_write_ratio.description: Warn if reads/writes drops below this for a non-trivial task.

context_pointers.description: Files the agent should read at task start to understand the repo. Keep this short — these are loaded eagerly.

design_references.description: Specific apps / sites whose UI the agent should imitate concrete values from (padding, radii, spacing, type ramp). Used by UI surfaces (mobile / web). Each entry pairs a source with WHAT specifically to mimic — vague references are useless.

url.description: URL or local path to screenshots / Figma file.

what.description: What specifically to imitate — exact numbers if known. e.g. 'Spacing rhythm: 12px card padding, 4px row gap, 24px section breaks'.

branching.description: Branching strategy. check_branching validates the repo against this.

required_branches.description: Branches that must exist on the remote. Default ['main', 'dev'].

default_branch.description: Branch new clones check out. Default 'main'.

feature_branch_pattern.description: Regex feature branches must match. Empty disables the check.

mode.description: soft: violations are warnings. hard: violations are errors.

ci.description: Required CI shape for this repo. check_ci_setup validates the workflow file matches.

kind.description: service: API/backend with deploy. library: code-only, no deploy. mobile: iOS/Android/Flutter, ships via app stores. web: Next.js/Vite SPA, deploys via Railway/Vercel.

bespoke.description: Set true when this repo intentionally uses a custom CI workflow instead of a canonical quality-gate-*.yml. Suppresses the CI_NO_CANONICAL_QUALITY_GATE finding. Use sparingly — bespoke CI drifts from org standards.

bespoke_reason.description: Required if bespoke: true. Document WHY this repo can't use the canonical workflow.

oneOf.description: Legacy plain-string rule. Treated as tier: practice. Schema v3 will deprecate this form.

rule.description: The rule text shown to agents.

tier.description: Enforcement tier. Principles do not live in this file — they live in CLAUDE.md.

id.description: Stable rule identifier for metrics and drift-log correlation.

check_command.description: Command, MCP tool, or CI job that enforces this rule. Format: 'mcp:tool_name', 'ci:job_name', or 'scripts/path.sh'.

owner.description: Person accountable for landing the check.

reason.description: Why the check isn't built yet.

deferred.description: Present when the rule is declared but its check_command isn't yet built. get_standards surfaces these as DEFERRED_INVARIANT_NO_CHECK info findings.

model.description: Logical model family. Concrete IDs (claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-6, etc.) live in CLAUDE.md so model upgrades don't require touching every project file.

effort.description: Reasoning effort. Higher = more thinking tokens, better quality on hard problems. Most useful on opus.
